hook: My desk lamp fix cost $25 and fixed everything

scenes.visual_prompt: Dim dorm desk corner with one harsh overhead fluorescent light and an outlet awkwardly placed behind a monitor
scenes.voiceover: Dorm desks are almost always undersized and under-lit — this one had one dim overhead light.

scenes.visual_prompt: Unboxing a black LED desk lamp with a built-in organizer base at the foot of the bed
scenes.voiceover: I added an LED desk lamp with a built-in organizer base instead of fighting the layout.

scenes.visual_prompt: Hand clicking through three color temperature settings on the lamp base, warm to cool light change visible on the desk
scenes.voiceover: Warm setting for night reading, cool setting for daytime focus — not a gimmick once you're up at 1am.

scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of the four small compartments in the lamp base holding pens, a phone, and a USB drive
scenes.voiceover: It won't replace a full desk organizer if you own a lot of stationery, but it holds the essentials.

scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot of the desk corner now with lamp, organizer base, and the folding velvet chair pulled up
scenes.voiceover: Paired with the folding velvet chair, this corner went from a place I avoided to a place I actually sit.

scenes.visual_prompt: Split screen: eyes looking tired under blue-white light vs relaxed under warm light
scenes.voiceover: The difference in how tired my eyes felt after two hours of studying was noticeable.

caption: The $25 lamp that fixed a desk corner I was ready to give up on. Full walkthrough (and what it doesn't fix) on the blog.
